title: Rotorua in June
meta_desc: Discover Rotorua in June with kids, enjoy geothermal wonders, Maori culture, and outdoor activities with a budget of $250-450/day for a family of four, suitable for all ages
intro: June is a fantastic time to visit Rotorua with kids, with mild winter weather and a range of exciting activities to enjoy. With a budget of $250-450/day for a family of four, you can experience the best of Rotorua's geothermal wonders, Maori culture, and outdoor adventures. From Te Puia to Skyline Rotorua, there's something for every age and interest.

sections.heading: Weather in June
sections.body:
June is winter in Rotorua, but the weather is generally mild, with average highs of 12°C and lows of 2°C. It's a great time to enjoy the geothermal parks, as the cooler weather makes the steam and hot springs even more appealing. Be sure to pack warm clothing, including jackets and gloves, as it can get chilly in the mornings and evenings.

With an average of 12 rainy days in June, it's a good idea to pack waterproof gear and check the weather forecast before heading out. However, the rain can also make the waterfalls and geothermal features even more spectacular, so it's not all bad news!

sections.heading: What's On in June
sections.body:
June is a great time to experience Rotorua's Maori culture, with several events and festivals taking place throughout the month. The Rotorua Maori Cultural Performance is a must-see, with traditional dances, songs, and stories. You can also visit the Te Puia Maori Arts and Crafts Institute to learn about Maori carving, weaving, and other traditional arts.

For outdoor enthusiasts, June is a great time to go hiking, mountain biking, or horse trekking in Rotorua's beautiful forests and parks. The Whakarewarewa Redwoods Forest is a popular spot, with its stunning trees and scenic trails.
list: Te Puia Maori Arts and Crafts Institute

sections.heading: Best Activities for June
sections.body:
June is a great time to visit Rotorua's geothermal parks, as the cooler weather makes the steam and hot springs even more appealing. Te Puia and Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland are must-visit attractions, with their stunning geothermal features and traditional Maori culture. The Agrodome Farm Show is another great activity, with its sheep shearing, cow milking, and other farm activities.

For adventure seekers, OGO Rotorua and Skyline Rotorua are great options, with their zorbing and luge rides offering an adrenaline-packed experience. And for a more relaxing activity, the Government Gardens and croquet lawn are a great spot for a picnic or a game of croquet.

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
sections.body:
When visiting Rotorua with kids, it's a good idea to book ahead for popular attractions like Te Puia and Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland. You can also consider purchasing a Rotorua attraction pass, which can save you money and time.

Be sure to pack warm clothing, including jackets and gloves, as it can get chilly in the mornings and evenings. And don't forget to bring waterproof gear, as June can be a rainy month.
list: Consider purchasing a Rotorua attraction pass
Check the weather forecast before heading out

sections.heading: Crowd Levels and Booking Ahead
sections.body:
June is a relatively quiet month in Rotorua, with smaller crowds and shorter queues for popular attractions. However, it's still a good idea to book ahead for popular activities like Te Puia and Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland, especially on weekends and school holidays.

Be sure to check the attraction's website or call ahead to confirm opening hours and prices, as these can change during the winter months.
